title: Listen and Choose a Response — Study Related

questions.stem: Does the library have copies of the required textbooks on reserve?

options.A: Yes, there are two copies on reserve that can be checked out for two hours.
options.B: Textbooks can be very expensive to buy new.
options.C: I bought my textbook from the campus store.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about textbook access at the library, so providing specific information about availability and loan periods is helpful.

questions.stem: I'm so nervous about the oral presentation. What if I forget what to say?

options.A: You'll do great! Just practice a few more times and bring notes as backup.
options.B: Oral presentations are a common requirement.
options.C: I gave my presentation last week.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing anxiety about a presentation, so offering reassurance and practical advice is supportive.

questions.stem: How do I book a group study room in the library?

options.A: You can reserve one online through the library website up to a week in advance.
options.B: Group study rooms have whiteboards and projectors.
options.C: I prefer studying in quiet areas.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about the booking process, so explaining how to reserve a room directly answers their question.

questions.stem: I think I submitted the wrong file for the assignment. I'm panicking.

options.A: Don't worry, email the professor right now and explain. Most are understanding.
options.B: Assignments should be checked before submission.

questions.explanation: The speaker is panicking about a submission error, so offering a calming and actionable solution is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: Which database should I use to find peer-reviewed articles on climate change?

options.A: Try JSTOR or ScienceDirect. Both have extensive environmental science collections.
options.B: Climate change research is published in many journals.
options.C: I used Google Scholar for my paper.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for research guidance, so recommending specific databases with a rationale is directly helpful.

questions.stem: You won't believe what happened — the professor walked into class with his shirt buttoned wrong!

options.A: No way! Did anyone tell him? That's hilarious.
options.C: I always check my clothes before leaving.

questions.explanation: The speaker is sharing an amusing anecdote, so reacting with surprise and engaging with the story is the natural social response.

questions.stem: Does the department prefer APA or MLA format for research papers?

options.A: Most of our faculty prefer APA format for the social sciences.
options.C: I use a citation generator tool.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a formatting requirement, so providing the department's preference directly answers their question.

questions.stem: Let's meet at the library at 10 AM tomorrow to work on the project.

options.A: Sounds good. I'll bring my laptop and the data we collected.
options.B: The library opens at 8 AM on weekdays.
options.C: I worked on the project yesterday.

questions.explanation: The speaker is proposing a meeting plan, so confirming the arrangement and stating what you'll bring is cooperative.
